behavior_summary: The item exposes two processes: a U8P_DEADEW::look handler (U8P_DEADEW::look) that presents a short descriptive bark ('dead body') and then suspends the interaction, and a U8P_DEADEW::use handler (U8P_DEADEW::use) that implements interaction logic for opening or blocking the item's UI.
U8P_DEADEW::use reads the item's status word (via Item::getStatus(this)) and tests bit 0x0100; if that bit is set the script closes any open UI for the item (Item::closeGump(this)) and returns.

If the 0x0100 bit is not set, U8P_DEADEW::use performs a low-probability random check (urandom(100) > 95). On that rare outcome it emits a curse bark 'A curse upon you, thief!' and suspends, otherwise it opens the item's gump UI with Item::openGump(0x0C, this).
notable_control_flow: U8P_DEADEW::look: immediate bark 'dead body' then suspend (terminates U8P_DEADEW::look flow). (confidence: high)

U8P_DEADEW::use: read status -> if (status & 0x0100) then Item::closeGump(this) and return; else perform urandom(0x64) > 0x5F check -> if true bark 'A curse upon you, thief!' and suspend; else Item::openGump(0x0C, this). (confidence: medium-high)
open_questions: What exact gameplay meaning is encoded by the status bit 0x0100 (e.g., locked, already-opened, protected)? (confidence: medium)
Is the curse bark intended to represent damage, status effect, or only a message? The script only barks then suspends, so side effects beyond the message are not shown. (confidence: low)
Are there other processes or global flags in the system that interact with this item's gump state not visible in this extract? (confidence: low)

main_function.name: U8P_DEADEW::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short examine/description text to the player when they inspect the item (dead body) and then end the interaction.
entry_conditions: Invoked when the player uses the 'U8P_DEADEW::look' / examine action on this item instance.

function_map.name: U8P_DEADEW::look
function_map.role: examine/description handler (displays short bark and suspends)

function_map.name: U8P_DEADEW::use
function_map.role: interaction handler (checks item status, optionally curses, opens/closes gump)

function_map.name: Item::getStatus
function_map.role: external API to read the item's status/flag word

function_map.name: Item::closeGump
function_map.role: external API to close the item's UI/gump

function_map.name: Item::openGump
function_map.role: external API to open a UI/gump for the item (gump id 0x0C used)

function_map.name: urandom
function_map.role: random number generator used to decide low-probability branch

function_map.name: suspend
function_map.role: engine operation to pause/terminate the current process after barks

external_calls.purpose: Read the item's status/flag word to test bit 0x0100 before deciding whether to open or close the gump.
Close any open UI for this item when the status bit 0x0100 indicates the item should not open its gump.
Produce a 0-99 random value to implement a low-probability curse branch (threshold >95).
Open the item's UI/gump (id 0x0C) when allowed by status and random check.
Terminate/pause the process after emitting the curse bark or at the end of U8P_DEADEW::look.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: The item exposes two processes: a U8P_DEADEW::look handler (U8P_DEADEW::look) that presents a short descriptive bark ('dead body') and then suspends the interaction, and a U8P_DEADEW::use handler (U8P_DEADEW::use) that implements interaction logic for opening or blocking the item's UI.
